_comment: SYNTHETIC target graph for the theory-eval harness, mirroring the REAL target_graph.json shape that compare_graphs.py reads: domains -> { package, components, entities }, components keyed by class NAME with { type, file_path }. This is the BASELINE 'hollow' graph: it CONTAINS the mapped class XService but carries NO rule-level evidence (the per-component rule-evidence array the fixed compare_graphs reads is absent everywhere).

_design.PROVES_T4: Under today's compare_graphs.py, REQ_X is marked PASS purely because a component named 'XService' exists -- even though XService implements ZERO of REQ_X's RULE-001/RULE-002/VAL-001/ERR-001. That is the hollow round-trip T4 exposes: 'done' = a class is spelled correctly, not that the rules are implemented.
_design.NO_RULE_EVIDENCE: Intentionally there is NO rule-evidence array on any component (the field compare_graphs reads to score coverage). The post-fix compare_graphs should compute coverage 0/4 for REQ_X against THIS graph -> PARTIAL/FAIL, never PASS. T4's PASS case supplies a separate inline/derived target graph that DOES carry rule evidence at full coverage.
_design.ENTITY_PRESENT: An XService-adjacent Customer entity is included so the graph is shaped like the real one; entities are not what the done-check turns on.
